mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month changes in industrial production positively predict S&P 500 20-day forward returns because manufacturing output fluctuations map directly into aggregate earnings revisions.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency during INDPRO expansion months is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 2.0% increases the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and compresses valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in consumer prices and the federal funds rate signal stagflationary monetary tightening that erodes real corporate profits, driving negative S&P 500 60-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: Interaction term adds less than 0.02 incremental R-squared over any 3-year rolling window, or negative return frequency in joint-up months is not significantly higher than in single-factor months.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, an unemployment rate that rises more than 0.2 percentage points over three months reflects deteriorating labor income that reduces consumer spending and triggers negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.2 percentage points over trailing 3 months
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of occurrences over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month changes in housing starts positively predict S&P 500 5-day forward returns because residential investment strength lifts construction and materials sector earnings.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months with rising HOUST over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a manufacturing recession that reduces aggregate corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter annualized real GDP growth above 2% signals robust aggregate demand that drives broad-based earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between GDPC1 growth and 60-day returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payrolls growth exceeding 200 thousand reflects labor market momentum that sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by more than 200 thousand in the latest month
failure_condition: Triggered months produce negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances over a 2-year rolling period.
